articles.title: Holcim Warns Labor Shortage Could Slow Europe Construction Recovery

articles.title: Kesko agrees €1.2bn deal for Dahl’s Nordic businesses
articles.summary: The three businesses posted combined net sales of €2.06bn in 2025, with Sweden contributing €922m, Norway €614m and Denmark €533m.

articles.title: Saint-Gobain Shares Rise on $1.76 Billion Sale of Majority of Dahl Operations
articles.summary: The stock rose after the company struck a deal to sell its specialist distribution business Dahl in three Nordic countries to the Finnish retailer Kesko.

articles.title: 3 European Dividend Stocks Yielding Up To 4.4%
articles.summary: As European markets experience a positive upswing, with the STOXX Europe 600 Index climbing by 3.00% amid hopes of geopolitical de-escalation, investors are increasingly eyeing dividend stocks as a potential source of steady income in an environment marked by economic uncertainties and revised growth forecasts. In such conditions, selecting dividend stocks that offer reliable payouts can be an attractive strategy for those looking to balance risk while capitalizing on market gains.

articles.title: Saint-Gobain shares down on softer 2026 margin outlook
articles.summary: Investing.com -- Shares in Compagnie de Saint Gobain SA (EPA:SGOB) fell more than 2% on Friday after the company guided for a 2026 EBITDA margin of “more than 15%”, below the 15.5% it delivered in 2025, overshadowing full-year results that slightly beat on operating income.

articles.title: Saint Gobain London Delisting Refocuses Liquidity Dividend And Growth Outlook
articles.summary: Compagnie de Saint-Gobain has delisted its ordinary shares from the London Stock Exchange. The company is keeping its primary listing on Euronext Paris under the ticker ENXTPA:SGO. The move affects where and how international investors can access the shares. Compagnie de Saint-Gobain, trading at €90.86 under ENXTPA:SGO, now concentrates its listing on Euronext Paris after stepping away from the London Stock Exchange.
